Study Hub
Study Hub / Pak Study / Testpoint / Lesson 10

Lesson 10

Questions 1801–2000 · Tap a card to reveal the explanation.

1

Khunjerab National Park is in _____?

Correct Answer: Pakistan
Show explanation
Explanation

Khunjerab National is a national park in Gilgit Baltistan, Pakistan.

Khunjerab National Park is Pakistan's third largest national park, and is adjacent to the Taxkorgan Natural Reserve in China.

2

Haft IX is a what type of weapon?

Correct Answer: Short-range ballistic missile
Show explanation
Explanation

Hatf IX (Nasr):

A short-range, surface-to-surface ballistic missile system.

Serves as a tactical nuclear weapon for battlefield use.

Developed by Pakistan’s National Development Complex (NDC).

Designed to counter advancing armored formations.

Can carry low-yield nuclear or conventional warheads.

Operational range is approximately 70 km.

3

The President of Pakistan cannot appoint which of the following?

Correct Answer: Chief Minister of a Province
Show explanation
Explanation

The Chief Minister is elected by the Provincial Assembly, not appointed by the President.  

However, the President does appoint the Chief Justice, COAS, and Governors, usually on the advice of the Prime Minister.

4

How much money per person is given to flood-hit people using the Watan Card?

Correct Answer: Rs. 20,000
Show explanation
Explanation

Under the Watan Card scheme, each flood-affected person received Rs. 20,000 as financial assistance from the government.

5

What is the recent annual inflation rate in Pakistan ?

Correct Answer: 6.24%
Show explanation
Explanation

The most recent annual inflation rate in Pakistan was 6.24% as of October 2025.

This is an increase from the previous month's rate of 5.61% and represents the highest rate in about a year. 

6

Jordan’s King Abdullah II was awarded which Pakistani award in 2025?

Correct Answer: Nishan-e-Pakistan
Show explanation
Explanation

Jordan’s King Abdullah II ibn Al Hussein made an official visit to Pakistan in November 2025.

He was awarded Nishan e Pakistan on 16 November 2025 by Pakistani government.

King Abdullah also conferred the Order of the Bejewelled Grand Cordon of Al-Nahda, one of the kingdom’s highest honours, upon President Zardari.

 

 Additional Info:

Jordan is a country in the Southern Levant region of West Asia.

****


7

Where are the biggest gold and copper mines in Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Baluchistan province
Show explanation
Explanation

Reko Diq represents one of the largest copper and gold reserves in the world 

The Reko Diq Mine is a planned mining operation, located near Reko Diq town in Chagai District

The biggest deposit of copper is present in Reko Diq, discovered in 1969.

Tethyan is seeking to develop the Reko Diq copper-gold deposit in the Chagai Hills

Reko Diq is a town in Balochistan.


8

Which is grown all over Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Wheat
Show explanation
Explanation

Wheat is a staple crop grown extensively across all provinces of Pakistan, making it one of the country's primary agricultural products.

It is cultivated in both the Rabi (winter) and Kharif (summer) seasons, contributing significantly to food security and the economy.

9

Pakistan Men's Hockey Team has won the Asian Games gold medal _____ times.

Correct Answer: 8
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Pakistan men's national field hockey team has won the Asian Games gold medal eight times: 1958, 1962, 1970, 1974, 1978, 1982, 1990, and 2010.
  • Pakistan is the only Asian team to win the Champions Trophy three times (1978 and 1980).
  • The team has won three Olympic gold medals,
  • Pakistan with their first in 1960, defeating India in the final.
    • 10

    Among the following, which is the oldest English language newspaper of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Dawn
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Dawn is the largest and oldest English-language newspaper in Pakistan.

    Quaid-e-Azam was the founder of Daily English Newspaper Dawn.

    Among the following, which is the oldest English language newspaper of Pakistan?

    ====

    Agency France-Presse (AFP) is the oldest news agency in the world.

    rk4323

    11

    What was Pakistan’s circular debt in the power sector as of March 2025?

    Correct Answer: Rs 2.396 trillion
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    According to reports, the stock of circular debt in the power sector in Pakistan stood at Rs 2.396 trillion by the end of March 2025

    Pakistan's power sector circular debt was around Rs. 2.636 trillion in early 2024 in May 2024.

    The debt then decreased to approximately Rs. 2.467 trillion by September 2024 and dropped further to about Rs. 1.6 trillion by mid-2025. 


    12

    Who is the current speaker of National Assembly of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Sardar Ayaz Sadiq
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Sardar Ayaz Sadiq is the current speaker of National Assembly of Pakistan.

    Appointing date: 01 March 2024

    Party name: PML-N

    13

    How many gold modals Pakistan won in the Common Wealth Games 2010 held in India?

    Correct Answer: 2
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan won 2 gold medals in the 2010 Commonwealth Games held in India, both in wrestling events.

    The Pakistan ranked 17th overall, with a total of 5 medals:

    2 gold,

    1 silver

     2 bronze.

    14

    When Pakistan won 1st hockey gold medal in Asian games?

    Correct Answer: 1958
    Show explanation
    Explanation
  • Pakistan men's national field hockey team has won the Asian Games gold medal eight times: 1958, 1962, 1970, 1974, 1978, 1982, 1990, and 2010.
  • Pakistan is the only Asian team to win the Champions Trophy three times (1978 and 1980).
  • The team has won three Olympic gold medals,
  • Pakistan with their first in 1960, defeating India in the final.
    • 15

    In recent series between Pakistan & New-Zealand, who Captain & Vice Captain were?

    Correct Answer: Salman Ali Agha (C) & Shadab Khan (VC)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan have dropped captain Mohammad Rizwan and Babar Azam for the T20 series against New Zealand and rewarded several domestic performers for the five-match series starting on March 16.

    Salman Ali Agha will lead the side with all-rounder Shadab Khan as the vice-captain.


    16

    All are the neighboring country of Pakistan? Tajikistan, Afghanistan , Iran , China

    Correct Answer: Tajikistan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Tajikistan is the country that does not share a border with Pakistan

    The neighboring countries of Pakistan are Afghanistan, Iran, China, and India.


    17

    Who is the President of The National Bank of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Rehmat Ali Hasnie
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The current President and CEO of the National Bank of Pakistan (NBP) is Rehmat Ali Hasnie.

    He has over three decades of experience and was appointed to this role in August 2023. 

    18

    Which party was involved in the Faizabad Dharna? PTI, PML-N, TLP, None of these

    Correct Answer: TLP
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Faizabad Dharna (2017) was led by Tehreek-e-Labbaik Pakistan (TLP).

    It was a protest over the amendment in the Election Act wording.

    19

    Who was Asma Jahangir?

    Correct Answer: A human rights lawyer
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Asma Jahangir passed away on February 11, 2018, at the age of 66, due to a cardiac arrest in Lahore, Pakistan.

    He was born on January 27, 1952.

    20

    The first Principal of Government College Lahore was _____?

    Correct Answer: Dr. G.W. Leitner
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Government College Lahore was established in 1864, and its first Principal was Dr. G.W. Leitner.

    He played a key role in setting the academic foundation of the institution.

    21

    When was Moen-Jo-Daro and Harappa (Indus Civilization Valleys) discovered?

    Correct Answer: 1922
    Show explanation
    Explanation
  • Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa were discovered in 1922 by R.D. Banerji and Daya Ram Sahni, respectively.
  • Large-scale excavations at Mohenjo-Daro were led by John Marshall and K. N. Dikshit.
    • 22

    ________ was the founder of the first Afghan dynasty in India.

    Correct Answer: Bahlol Khan Lodi
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Lodhi Dynasty was established by the Bahlul Khan Lodhi in India in the year 1451.

    23

    When did the second battle of Panipat take place?

    Correct Answer: 5 November 1556
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    First Battle of Panipat: 21 April 1526

    Fought Between: Babur Vs Lodi

    Won by: Babur

    Second Battle of Panipat: November 5, 1556

    Fought Between: Hemu Vs Akbar

    Won by: Akbar

    Third Battle of Panipat: 14 January 1761

    Fought Between: Abdali Vs Marathas

    Won by: Abdali


    24

    CPEC (China-Pakistan Economic Corridor) is supporting which motorway?

    Correct Answer: M5 Motorway
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) is supporting the Sukkur-Multan Motorway (M5) and the Hakla-D.I Khan Motorway, among other projects.

    The M5 is a major completed project under CPEC, while the Hakla-D.I Khan section of the Peshawar-Karachi Motorway is also part of CPEC and is under construction.

    The Sukkur-Hyderabad Motorway (M-6) is also being considered for inclusion in CPEC.

    25

    Who is called the "Traveler of Islam"?

    Correct Answer: Ibn-e-Battute
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ibn Battuta was a Muslim Berber-Moroccan scholar, jurist, and explorer who widely traveled the Old World, largely in the lands of Dar al-Islam

    The full name of Ibn Battuta was Muhammad Ibn Battuta.

    He was born on February 25, 1304

    Died: 1369, Marrakesh, Morocco

    Place of burial: Tomb of Ibn Battuta, Tangier, Morocco

     his extensive travels covering some 75,000 miles (120,000 km) in trips to almost all of the Muslim countries and as far as China and Sumatra 


    ND23-4-2023

    26

    Pakistan Environment and Protection Agency is now called?

    Correct Answer: PEPA
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Pakistan Environment and Protection Agency is now called the Pakistan Environmental Protection Agency, often abbreviated as Pak-EPA.

    The agency operates under the Pakistan Environmental Protection Act (PEPA) 1997. 

    Formed November 9, 1997

    27

    Where is the Fasting Buddha housed?

    Correct Answer: Lahore Museum
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Fasting Siddhartha or Fasting Buddha.  

    Location: Housed in the Lahore Museum, Pakistan.  

    Origin: Discovered in the Gandhara region, an ancient Buddhist center.  

    Material: Made of grey schist (stone).  

    Period: Belongs to the 2nd–3rd century CE, from the Kushan Empire period.  

    Depiction: Shows Buddha during his severe asceticism, before attaining enlightenment.  

     Significance: Symbolizes sacrifice, discipline, and spiritual determination in Buddhism.


    28

    The Legal Framework Order (LFO) issued in:

    Correct Answer: 1970
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Legal Framework Order, 1970  was issued by the President of Pakistan Muhammad Yahya Khan.

     The LFO also dissolved the "One Unit" scheme of West Pakistan.

    29

    The period of 1947 to 1953 of Pakistan is known as era of _______?

    Correct Answer: Neutrality
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    1947–1953: Neutrality,

    1954–1962: Alliances,

    1963–1978: Bilateralism,

    1979–1989: Alliances,

    1990–present: Post-Cold War.

    30

    The M1 Motorway in Pakistan was constructed under which project?

    Correct Answer: Pakistan Motorway Project
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The M1 Motorway is part of the Pakistan Motorway Project (also called the National Trade Corridor Project).

    Officially named the Islamabad–Peshawar Motorway.

    Construction started in 1997 under Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif but halted in 1999.

    Project resumed in 2003 under President Pervez Musharraf, with the contract awarded to PMC-JV.

    Inaugurated in October 2007.

    31

    Which cricketer is known as Rawalpindi Express?

    Correct Answer: Shoaib Akhtar
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Shoaib Akhtar is a Pakistani former international cricketer and commentator.

    Nicknamed the "Rawalpindi Express"

    He is the fastest bowler in cricketing history, with an unbeaten 161.3 kmph (100.23 mph) delivery.

    He made his Test match debut in November 1997 as an opening fast bowler

    Born: 13 August 1975, Rawalpindi

    Starting his List A career during the 1993/1994 season and his first-class career in 1994/1995

    32

    The article 370 of Indian constitution is related to ______?

    Correct Answer: Special status to Kashmir
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    India revoked Article 370 of its constitution on 5 August 2019. 

    Article 370 of the Indian constitution gave special status to Jammu and Kashmir

    Jammu and Kashmir was administered by India as a state from 17 November 1952 to 31 October 2019

    Article 370 was drafted in Part XXI of the Indian constitution titled "Temporary, Transitional and Special Provisions"

    33

    Who is known as the Father of the Nation in Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Muhammad Ali Jinnah
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Muhammad Ali Jinnah is known as the Father of the Nation in Pakistan

    ***

    ***

    Title of Quaid e Azam was given to Mohammad Ali Jinnah by Maulana Mazharuddin Shaheed in 1938

    Mohammed Ali Jinnah died on September 11, 1948, in Karachi, Pakistan.

    He was the founder and first governor-general (1947–48) of Pakistan

    He was Born: December 25, 1876, Karachi

    Spouse: Rattanbai Jinnah (m. 1918–1929), Emibai Jinnah (m. 1892–1893)

    Siblings: Fatima Jinnah, Shireen Jinnah, Maryam Bai Jinnah, Ahmad Ali Jinnah, Bunde Ali Jinnah, Rahmat Ali Jinnah

    Children: Dina Wadia

    Parents: Poonja Jinnah, Mithibai Jinnah

    Quaid-e-Azam Residency also known as Ziarat Residency

    Quaid-e-Azam Residency is located in Ziarat, Balochistan, Pakistan

    34

    What is Roshan Samaaji Khidmat?

    Correct Answer: A one-stop digital platform for NRPs to donate and pay Zakat in Pakistan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Roshan Samaaji Khidmat allows Non-Resident Pakistanis (NRPs) with a Roshan Digital Account to donate, pay Zakat, and fulfill Qurbani obligations digitally. 

    35

    Operation Zarb-e-Azab was started in which year?

    Correct Answer: June 15, 2014
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Operation Zarb-e-Azb, started by Army Chief General Raheel Sharif, ran from June 15, 2014, to February 22, 2017, in Pakistan.
    • It including North Waziristan, FATA, Karachi, Balochistan, and Punjab.
    • Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad, launched on February 22, 2017.
    • It is an intensive nationwide operation against terror groups led by Army Chief General Qamar Javed Bajwa.
    36

    India revoked the special status granted under Indian constitution of Jammu Kashmir on?

    Correct Answer: 5 August 2019
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On 5 August 2019, the Parliament of India voted in favour of a resolution tabled by Home Minister Amit Shah to revoke the temporary special status, or autonomy, granted under Article 370 of the Indian Constitution to Jammu and Kashmir


    AGM 02.05.2023


    37

    The Crusades had which major effect on the Muslim world?

    Correct Answer: Rise of military leadership like Salahuddin & cultural interactions
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Crusades had which major effect on the Muslim world  Rise of military leadership like Salahuddin & cultural interactions. 

    The Crusades led to the emergence of powerful and charismatic military and political leaders such as Nur al-Din Zangi and Saladin,

    Who mobilized a unified Muslim response to the Crusader states and became celebrated heroes.

    The period also involved extensive, though often complex, cultural and commercial interactions.


    38

    Which operation did Pakistani Army launch to response Indian aggression in May 2025?

    Correct Answer: Operation Bunyan Al Marsoos
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan launched precision missile strikes against India under Operation Bunyan Um Marsoos.

    Bunyan Ul Marsoos is an Arabic verse from the Quran.

    The operation’s name—meaning “a structure firmly joined together”—draws inspiration from Surah Al-Saff (61-4), which praises those who fight in Allah’s cause as a solid, fortified structure.

    إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الَّذِينَ يُقَاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِهِ صَفًّا كَأَنَّهُم بُنْيَانٌ مَّرْصُوصٌ

    Translation: ‘Allah indeed loves those who fight in His Way as though they are a solid wall cemented with molten lead’ (Surah Al-Saff, verse 4).

    This operation was launched on 10 May 2025 (after Fajr prayer)

    Seven sites in India were targeted, including the key Pathankot air base, Udhampur air base, and Gujarat air base. Rajasthan air base and Brahmos storage site.

    The operation was launched in retaliation for Indian missile strikes that hit several locations inside Pakistan.

     ****

    39

    The Round Table Conferences of 1930s were called for _____?

    Correct Answer: The future constitution of India
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Round Table Conferences (1930–1932) were held in London to discuss the future constitution of India.

    They aimed to negotiate political reforms between the British government and Indian leaders.

    40

    A senator is elected in Upper House of Pakistan for _____?

    Correct Answer: 6 years
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    A senator is elected in Pakistan for 6 Years

    Senate: Upper House of Pakistan

    Senate in Urdu: ایوان بالا

    Formation: 1973

    Original members: 45

    Current members: 96

    Half of the Senate members retire after 3 years

    The Head of the Senate is called: Chairman

    National Assembly & Senate: Makes up the Parliament of Pakistan

    Seats for each province: 23

    The Senate of Pakistan ایوان بالا is the upper legislative chamber of the bicameral legislature of Pakistan.

    **********


    41

    Who was the fourth Governor-General of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Iskander Mirza
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    List of Governors-General of Pakistan
    Term Name Birth–Death Monarch Took office Left office Political party Remarks
    1 Muhammad Ali Jinnah (1876–1948) George VI
    (1947–1952)    		 15-Aug-47 11-Sep-48 Pakistan Muslim League died in office
    2 Sir Khawaja Nazimuddin (1894–1964) = 14-Sep-48 17-Oct-51 Pakistan Muslim League became prime minister
    3 Sir Ghulam Muhammad (1895–1956) = 17-Oct-51 7-Aug-55 Independent dismissed
    4 Iskander Mirza (1899–1969) Elizabeth II
    (1952–1956)    		 7-Aug-55 23-Mar-56 Republican Party became president
    42

    The lowest point of altitude in Pakistan is _______?

    Correct Answer: Arabian Sea
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The lowest point in Pakistan is at sea level along the Arabian Sea coast.

    Coastal areas like Karachi and Gwadar lie at this lowest altitude.

    43

    Which of the following is a national park in Balochistan? Lal Suhaara National Park, Hangul National Park, Kirthar National Park, None of these

    Correct Answer: Hangul National Park
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hingol National Park is the largest national park in Pakistan, located in the Makran coastal region.

    The park covers an area of about 6,100 square kilometres (2,400 sq mi).

    It is located 190 km from Karachi in the three districts of Gwadar, Lasbela and Awaran in Balochistan.

    Hingol was declared a national park in 1988.

    44

    This year which of the following Pakistani Woman has climbed the Mount Everest?

    Correct Answer: Naila Kiani
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On 14 May 2023, Naila Kiani becomes second Pakistani woman to scale Mount Everest

    All about Mount Everest


    Mount Everest is the Highest peak in the world.

    The highest part of the Earth is Mount Everest

    It is located in Nepal.

    The new height of Mount Everest is 8,848.86m (29,032 feet).

    Mount Everest was named after George Everest in 1856

    First time Mount Everest expedition in 1953

    Mount Everest is present in the range of mountains: the Himalayas

    *****

    the first person to reach Mount Everest: 

    Sherpa Tenzing and Edmund Percival Hillary

    ****

    The first man to climb Mount Everest twice was: 

    Nawang Gombu

    ***

    The first Pakistani woman to scale Mount Everest is:

    Samina Baig

    ***

     the first blind man in Asia and the third in the world to climb Mount Everest:

    Zhang Hong
    ****
    the youngest Pakistani to summit Mount Everest (8,848m), the tallest peak in the world:
    Sheroze Kashif
    ****

    Who has made a world record and become the fastest in the women's category to Scale Mount Everest?

    Tsang Yin-hung
    45

    Which of the following is situated in the Cholistan Desert? Rohtas Fort, Ranikot Fort, Derawar Fort, None of these

    Correct Answer: Derawar Fort
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Derawar Fort is a historic fort located in the Cholistan Desert, Bahawalpur.

    It is famous for its massive square structure and 40 bastions, visible from miles away.

    46

    Which Ocean is in the south of Pakistan? Atlantic Ocean, Indian Ocean, Arctic Ocean, None of these

    Correct Answer: Indian Ocean
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Indian Ocean lies to the south of Pakistan, along its coastal areas like Karachi and Gwadar.

    It connects Pakistan to major international sea routes.

    47

    GT road stands for _____?

    Correct Answer: Grand Trunk Road
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    GT RoadDetail
    Full Form Grand Trunk Road
    Constructed  bySher Shah Suri
    Sher Shah Suri Real nameFareed Khan
    48

    Where is Satpara Lake situated?

    Correct Answer: Gilgit Baltistan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Satpara Lake is a natural lake near Skardu, Gilgit-Baltistan.

    It is fed by the Satpara Stream.

    Satpara Lake is situated at an elevation of 2,636 meters above sea level and is spread over an area of 2.5 km².

    49

    The Army Desert Warfare School is located in _______?

    Correct Answer: Chor Cantt
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Pakistan Army Desert Warfare School (ADWS) is based in Chhor Cantonment, Sindh.

    It was established in 1980 (as a wing) and upgraded to a full desert warfare school in 1987.

    50

    Asia’s largest RO (Reverse Osmosis) plant is located in ______?

    Correct Answer: Mithi
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Asia’s largest RO plant is located in Missri Shah, Mithi, Tharparkar.

    It supplies clean water to the desert region of Thar.

    Built at a cost of 300 million rupees.

    Has a production capacity of 2 million gallons of water per day.

    51

    Which Pakistani Urdu poet was given "Lenin Award' by the ex-USSR Government?

    Correct Answer: Faiz Ahmad Faiz
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Faiz was the first Asian poet to be awarded the Lenin Peace Prize (1962) by the Soviet Union 

    was also nominated for the Nobel Prize in literature.

    Faiz Ahmad Faiz is also the author of Urdu and Punjabi literature.

    Born: February 13, 1911, Narowal

    Died: November 20, 1984, in Lahore

    while 

    Name only two Pakistani who got Lenin Prize.

    Faiz Ahmad Faiz revived Lenin Prize in 1962.

     Abdul Satar Edhi revived Lenin Prize in 1988.

    ND28-4-2023

    52

    PIA stands for _____?

    Correct Answer: Pakistan International Airlines
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    Pakistan International Airlines
    It is an international airline that serves as the national flag carrier of Pakistan.
    Founded: 1946
    Headquarters: Karachi
    53

    This year on Pakistan Day, President Arif Alvi awarded Nishan-i-Imtiaz to famous sportsmen _____?

    Correct Answer: Jahangir Khan and Jansher Khan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On Pakistan Day 23 March 2023, President Arif Alvi awarded the Nishan-i-Imtiaz to the sports legends Jahangir Khan and Jansher Khan. 

    They are both squash champions who have greatly contributed to Pakistan’s sporting legacy.

    54

    Name the most important demand, which was presented in Simla Deputation for Muslims of India?

    Correct Answer: Separate Electorate
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Simla Deputation organized by the Indian Muslim leaders met the Governor-General and Viceroy Lord Minto in Simla on 1 October 1906. 

    The delegation of Simla deputation met Lord Minto on 1st October 1906

    Simla's deputation met with لارڈ منٹو

    شملہ وفد 1906

    1. شملہ وفد یکم اکتوبر 1906 کو وائسرائے ہند سے ملا.

    2. شملہ وفد کا صدر سر آغا خان تھا.

    3. اس میں 35 لوگ / ارکان شامل تھے.

    4. سب سے پہلے شملہ وفد ایم اے علی گڑھ کالج کے پرنسپل سے ملا.

    5. پر. نسپل نے سیکرٹری وائسرائے کرنل ڈونلپ سے یہ میٹنگ طے کر دیا، ان ہی کےذریعے وفد وائسرائے لارڈ منٹو سے ملا.

    6. چونکہ مسلمانوں کا یہ وفد وائسرائے ہند سے شملہ کے مقام پر ملا، اس وجہ سے اسکا نام شملہ وفد بن گیا.


    55

    When Quaid-e-Azam presented his historical fourteen points?

    Correct Answer: March 1929
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Jinnah gave his points in order to safeguard and protect the rights and interests of Muslims in March 1929.

    Muhammad Ali Johar helped Quaid e Azam in preparation of fourteen points 

    Nehru report presented in 1928

              List of Jinnah's fourteen points (presents in 1929 in response of )

     
    Title                               Description
    Point 1:Federal System
    Point 2:Provincial Autonomy
    Point 3:Representation of Minorities
    Point 4:Number of Muslim Representative
    Point 5:Separate Electorates
    Point 6:Muslim Majority Provinces
    Point 7:Religious Liberty
    Point 8:Three-Fourth Representation
    Point 9:Separation of Sindh
    Point 10:Introduction of Reforms in N.W.F.P and Baluchistan
    Point 11:Government Services
    Point 12:Protection of Muslim’s culture and Language
    Point 13:One-Third Muslim Ministers
    Point 14:

    Constitution

    56

    The Montagu-Chelmsford report formed the basis of Indian Council Act as?

    Correct Answer: The government of India act 1919
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The Montagu-Chelmsford Report was published on July 8, 1918.
    • The report was the result of discussions between Edwin Montagu, secretary of state for India from 1917 to 1922, and Lord Chelmsford, the viceroy of India between 1916 and 1921.
    • The report was used as the basis for the Government of India Act of 1919.
    57

    The mountain peak Takhat-e-Sulaiman is located in which province?

    Correct Answer: Balochistan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Takhat-e-Sulaiman peak Elevation 3,487 m.
    Sulaiman Mountains First ascender     George White.

    Takhat-e-Sulaiman peak is located in Balochsitan.


    The Takht-e-Sulaiman peak is located on the border of both the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa and Balochistan provinces in Pakistan. The peak is situated in the Sulaiman Mountains, near the town of Darazinda and the borders with both South Waziristan and Zhob.

    58

    On Pakistan Day 2023, President Arif Alvi awarded Governor Sindh?

    Correct Answer: Tamgha-i-Imtiaz
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On 23 March 2023 (Pakistan Day) President of Pakistan Dr. Arif Alvi awarded Kamran Tesori, governor Sindh with Tamgha-i-Imtiaz.

    Kamran Khan Tessori became governor Sindh on 09 October 2022.

    59

    Which of the following river flows both in Afghanistan and Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Kabul
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Kabul River empties into the Indus River near Attock, Pakistan.

    It is the main river in eastern Afghanistan and the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province of Pakistan.

    60

    Which is the longest day of the year in Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: June 21
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    22 December night is the longest night of the year.

    21 June day  the longest day of the year.


    61

    Which country observes Kashmir Day on February 5th every year?

    Correct Answer: Pakistan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Kashmir Solidarity Day or Kashmir Day is a national holiday observed in Pakistan 

    Date: February 5

    Kashmir Day Event Length: 1 Day

    Kashmir Day Related to the Kashmir Conflict

    Kashmir Day was Started by: Jamaat-e-Islami Pakistan in 1990

    Kashmir Day was first proposed by Qazi Hussain Ahmad 

    In 1991, the then-Prime Minister of Pakistan Nawaz Sharif called for a "Kashmir Solidarity Day Strike"


    62

    The Green Railway Line in Pakistan runs from where to where?

    Correct Answer: Karachi to Islamabad
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Green Line is an express train operated daily by Pakistan Railways between Karachi and Islamabad.

    It is the flagship rail of the organization and has the highest priority for passage.

    The train takes approximately 21 hours to cover a published distance of 1,521 kilometres (945 mi),

    Mian Muhammad Nawaz Sharif inaugurated it on 15 May 2015 at the Islamabad railway station.

    63

    When did Sindh Muslim League pass a resolution in favor of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: 1938
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On 10 October 1938, the Sindh Provincial Muslim League passed a resolution in favor of Pakistan at its session in Karachi.

    The session was presided over by Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah, marking the first formal provincial demand for Pakistan.

    64

    In March 1940 at Lahore, The Muslim League held it's first wartime meeting attended by almost 100,000 People here. The Lahore Resolution later known as what?

    Correct Answer: Pakistan resolution
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Lahore Resolution was passed in the 27th annual session of the All India Muslim League.

    *****

    Presented            22 March 1940

    Ratified                 23 March 1940

    Location              Lahore

    Author(s)             Muhammad Zafarullah Khan

    Presented            A.K Fazlulhuq

    Signatories          All-India Muslim League Purpose To announce the declaration of                             independence from British India.

    ****

    Lahore Resolution Translation English to Urdu: Mulana Zafar Ali Khan

    Lahore Resolution Presented by A.K Fazul Haq        

    Participate From Punjab (Leader): Zafar Ali Khan

    Participate From Sindh (Leader): Sir Abdullah Haroon

    Participate From Balochistan (Leader): Qazi Faiz Isa

    Participate From KPK (Leader): Sardar Aurangzaib Khan

    CM Banglad : A.K Fazul Haq 

    United Province: Khalid Zaman

    RK271222

    65

    Which of the following Pakistani cities is famous as "the City of Guava"?

    Correct Answer: Larkana
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The best guava is located in Larkana city۔

    Larkana is also called city of martyrs.

    Larkana is located in Sindh.

    66

    Which of these following was given Nishan-e-Haidar? Maj. Aziz Bhatti, Col. Shahid Aziz, Capt. Salman Farooq Lodhi, None of these

    Correct Answer: Maj. Aziz Bhatti
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Nishan-e-Haider literally means Emblem of Lion.

    1. Muhammad Sarwar Punjab Regiment, Pakistan Army Captain Indo-Pakistani War of 1947 27 July 1948

    2. Saif Ali Janjua 5 Azad Kashmir regiment (HAIDER DIL)/18 Azad Kashmir Regiment, Pakistan Army Naik Indo-Pakistani War of 1947 26 October 1948

    3. Tufail Mohammad 13 Punjab Regiment, Border Guards East Pakistan Rifles, Pakistan Army Major Indo-Pakistani border skirmishes 1958 7 August 1958

    4. Raja Aziz Bhatti 17 Punjab Regiment, Pakistan Army Major Indo-Pakistani War of 1965 10 September 1965

    5.  Rashid Minhas No. 2 Fighter Conversion Unit, Pakistan Air Force Pilot Officer Indo-Pakistani War of 1971 20 August 1971

    6. Muhammad Akram 4 Frontier Force Regiment, Pakistan Army Major Indo-Pakistani War of 1971 5 December 1971

    7. Shabbir Sharif 6 Frontier Force Regiment, Pakistan Army Major Indo-Pakistani War of 1971 6 December 1971

    8. Muhammad Hussain 20 Lancers (Armoured Corps), Pakistan Army Sowar Indo-Pakistani War of 1971 10 December 1971

    9. Muhammad Mahfuz Janjua 15 Punjab Regiment, Pakistan Army Lance naik Indo-Pakistani War of 1971 17 December 1971

    10. Karnal Sher Khan 12 Northern Light Infantry, Pakistan Army Captain Kargil War 5 July 1999

    11. Lalak Jan 12 Northern Light Infantry, Pakistan Army Havildar Kargil War 7 July 1999

    67

    The appointment of Chief Justice of Pakistan (CJP) is made by?

    Correct Answer: President
    Show explanation
    Explanation
  • The Chief Justice and Supreme Court judges are appointed by the President of Pakistan.
  • Appointments are made under Article 175A.
  • Article 175A was introduced through the 18th and 19th Constitutional Amendments.
    • 68

    Quetta , Loralai , and Lasbella  are cities in which province of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Balochistan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Quetta, Loralai, and Lasbela are cities located in the province of Balochistan

    Loralai is also known as Bori (Pashto: بورۍ)

    It is 4,700 feet (1,400 m) above sea level.

    *

    Quetta is the capital and largest city of the Pakistani province of Balochistan. 

    It is the ninth-largest city in Pakistan

    *

    Lasbela is a district in the province of Balochistan, Pakistan.

    The administrative headquarters for the Lasbela district is located in the city of Uthal. 

    69

    Which ancient civilization is located in Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Indus Valley Civilization
    Show explanation
    Explanation
  • The Indus Valley Civilization (3300–1300 BCE) was one of the world's earliest urban civilizations.
  • It is with major sites in present-day Pakistan, like Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa.

  • It was known for advanced city planning, drainage systems, and trade networks.
    • 70

    In how many natural regions Pakistan can be divided?

    Correct Answer: Six
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pakistan is divided into six natural regions:

    1. Northern mountain region
    2. Mountain of Western frontier
    3. Plato photo war and salt range
    4. The plate of Balochistan
    5. Upper Indus plain
    6. Low Indus plain

    قدرتی خطوں کے لحاظ سے پاکستان کو چھ حصوں میں تقسیم کیا گیا ہے: پہاڑی علاقہ، بالائی سندھ، بلوچستان، تھل، تھر اور میدان۔


    There are 3 Geographical regions of Pakistan:

    1. Northern highlands
    2. The Balochistan plateau
    3. The Indus River plain


    71

    Rawal lake is situated in which city of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Islamabad
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Rawal Lake  in Pakistan is an artificial reservoir that provides the water needs for the cities of Rawalpindi and Islamabad.

    72

    In Balochistan which is the largest ethnic group?

    Correct Answer: Balochi
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The largest ethnic group in Balochistan is the Baloch, comprising approximately 55.7% of the population.

    The second-largest group is the Pashtun, who form around 36% of the population.

    Other smaller communities include the Hazara, Sindhi, Punjabi, and Saraiki. 

    73

    Quaid-e-Millat is the title of which politician?

    Correct Answer: Liaquat Ali Khan
    Show explanation

    Liaquat Ali Khan was given the title:

    • Quaid-e-Millat (Leader of the Nation)
    • Shaheed-e-Millat (Martyr of the Nation)

    **

    Liaquat joined the All-India Muslim League in 1923.

    He was elected to the Legislative Council in 1926 

    He was the first PM of Pakistan (1947-51)

    He was Born on October 1, 1895, Karnal, India

    He was assassinated on October 16, 1951, in Rawalpindi

    The Place of burial: Mazar-e-Quaid, Jinnah's Mausoleum, Karachi

    What is the total length of Pakistan India boundary line?

    Correct Answer: 2100 km
    Show explanation

    75

    Karakorum highway connect Pakistan with which country?

    Correct Answer: China
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Silk Route is also known as Karakoram Highway

    The silk road connects Pakistan and China.

    The total length is approximately 1,300 kilometers, of which 887 kilometers are in Pakistan and 413 km in China.

    The silk road is considered a symbol of Pakistan-China's eternal friendship

    The highway meets the Pakistani-Chinese border at Khunjerab Pass

     It was started in 1959 and was completed and opened to the public in 1979.

    Karakorum Highway is located in the Karakoram range

    Karakorum highway in Pakistan starts from Hassan Abdal

    Karakorum highway is also called N-35

    ****

    ND23-2-2023

    Mother: Imam Bibi

    Received PhD: Germany

    Title: Poet of the East

    Famous books: Bang-e-Dara, Zarb-i-Kalim, Armughan-e-Hijaz

    Who became first South Asian to make biggest throw and win Javelin Gold in CWG 2022?

    Correct Answer: Arshad Nadeem
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The first javelin gold for Pakistan was won by Arshad Nadeem in Common Wealth Games in Birmingham, England in August 2022.

    ***

    • Arshad Nadeem won a gold medal for his record-breaking 90.18-meter throw.
    • Arshad Nadeem became the first South Asian and second Asian to win this title.

    ***

    •  Arshad Nadeem is a Pakistani javelin thrower.
    • He represents WAPDA in the domestic competition.
    • He is the first Pakistani to qualify for the final at the Olympic Games and the World Athletics Championships. 
    • Born: January 2, 1997 (age 25 years), Khanewal

    ***

    79

    Which of the following was most stricken by the floods in 2022? Balochistan, Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa, Sindh, None of these

    Correct Answer: Sindh
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Economic Impact: Sindh suffered 70% of Pakistan’s total flood-related losses and damages.

    Population Affected: Over 14.5 million people affected in 23 districts; 33 million affected nationwide.

    Infrastructure Damage: 2 million+ houses, 20,000 schools, 1,000 health facilities damaged; 64% of total road damage occurred in Sindh.

    Agricultural Damage: 4.8 million acres of crops inundated, affecting cotton, rice, and sugarcane.

    Public Health: Nearly half of deaths and 66% of injuries in Sindh; rise in waterborne diseases like malaria.

    Water Inundation: By mid-August 2022, one-third of Sindh was under floodwater, lasting several months in some areas.

    80

    Which of the following is the newest district of Sindh? Keamari, Badin, Matiari, None of these

    Correct Answer: Keamari
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Keamari District is the newest district in Sindh province, Pakistan.

    It was officially established in August 2020.

    The district was created by bifurcating Karachi West District.

    Keamari became the seventh district of Karachi.

    Sindh Cabinet approved the creation of Keamari District on August 20, 2020.

    Chief Minister Murad Ali Shah’s spokesperson announced the approval.

    Keamari is the 30th district of Sindh.

    The district includes Site, Baldia, Harbour, and Maripur sub-divisions.

    Karachi West District, from which Keamari was carved, is the largest district in Sindh by population.

    Karachi West District had a population of 3,914,757.

    81

    What is the capital of Gilgit Baltistan?

    Correct Answer: Gilgit city
    Show explanation
    Explanation

      Capital of Gilgit-Baltistan is Gilgit city

      Gilgit Baltistan is located on the North side of Pakistan

      Gilgit is a city of: Gilgit-Baltistan Pakistan

      Gilgit-Baltistan, formerly known as the Northern Areas, is a region administered by Pakistan as an administrative territory.

      Gilgit-Baltistan was Established on 1 November 1948

      Largest city of Gilgit-Baltistan is Skardu

      1. Muhammad Sarwar Punjab Regiment, Pakistan Army Captain Indo-Pakistani War of 1947 27 July 1948

      2. Saif Ali Janjua 5 Azad Kashmir regiment (HAIDER DIL)/18 Azad Kashmir Regiment, Pakistan Army Naik Indo-Pakistani War of 1947 26 October 1948

      3. Tufail Mohammad 13 Punjab Regiment, Border Guards East Pakistan Rifles, Pakistan Army Major Indo-Pakistani border skirmishes 1958 7 August 1958

      4. Raja Aziz Bhatti 17 Punjab Regiment, Pakistan Army Major Indo-Pakistani War of 1965 10 September 1965

      5.  Rashid Minhas No. 2 Fighter Conversion Unit, Pakistan Air Force Pilot Officer Indo-Pakistani War of 1971 20 August 1971

      7. Shabbir Sharif 6 Frontier Force Regiment, Pakistan Army Major Indo-Pakistani War of 1971 6 December 1971

      83

      Which is the biggest textile market in Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Faisalabad
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Faisalabad is the largest textile market in Pakistan.

      It is known as the "Manchester of Pakistan" due to its significant textile industry.

      Gadoon Textile Mills Limited, established in 1988.

      It is one of the largest spinning units in the country.

      84

      Pakistan's aim for Electric Vehicle (EV) sales by 2030 is _____?

      Correct Answer: 30%
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan's National Electric Vehicle (NEV) Policy 2025 aims for 30% of all new vehicle sales to be electric by 2030.

      This policy is a strategic plan to promote green transportation, reduce reliance on imported fuels, lower air pollution, and foster economic growth through new jobs and the development of an EV industry.

      The government is introducing incentives and fast-tracking processes to support this transition. 

      85

      The naval exercise between China and Pakistan is known as _____?

      Correct Answer: Sea Guardian
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The joint naval exercise between China and Pakistan is primarily known as Sea Guardian.

      The air force exercise is called Shaheen (Eagle). 

      86

      In 2019, the state of Jammu & Kashmir was reorganized and bifurcated into which of the following?

      Correct Answer: Two separate Union Territories
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Reorganization (2019):

      The state of Jammu & Kashmir was reorganized and bifurcated into two Union Territories.

      New Union Territories:

      Union Territory of Jammu & Kashmir (with a legislative assembly)

      Union Territory of Ladakh (without a legislature)

      Legal Basis:

      Enacted by the Jammu and Kashmir Reorganisation Act, 2019, passed by the Indian Parliament.

      Effective Date:

      Came into effect on October 31, 2019.

      Constitutional Change:

      Included the revocation of Article 370 of the Indian Constitution, which had granted special autonomous status to the region.

      87

      The armed conflict between Pakistan and India in 2025 lasted for _____?

      Correct Answer: 4
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Duration: The armed conflict between Pakistan and India lasted four days, from May 7 to May 10, 2025.

      Conclusion: Hostilities ended after a ceasefire agreement was reached.

      Means of Agreement: Ceasefire was negotiated via hotline communication between the Directors General of Military Operations (DGMOs) of both countries.

      Diplomatic Involvement: The United States contributed to diplomatic efforts to de-escalate the situation.

      88

      Nizamuddin Auliya was associated with which Sufi order?

      Correct Answer: Chishti
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Muhammad Nizamuddin Auliya:
      • Also known as Hazrat Nizamuddin and Mahbub-e-Ilahi.
      • Indian Sunni Muslim scholar and Sufi saint of the Chishti Order.
      • One of the most famous Sufis of the Indian Subcontinent.
      Baba Fariduddin Ganj-e-Shakar (Baba Farid):
      • Disciple of Qutbuddin Bhaktiyar Kaki (namesake of Qutab Minar).
      • Settled in Ajodhan, Punjab, and built a Jamaat Khana.
      Three disciples founded sub-orders:
      1. Nizamiyya order – founded by Nizamuddin Auliya.
      2. Jamaliyya order – founded by Jamaluddin Hanovi.
      3. Sabaria order – founded by Alauddin Sabin.
      89

      What is the name of the proposed Russia-Pakistan gas pipeline project?

      Correct Answer: Pakistan Stream Gas Pipeline
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Russia-Pakistan gas pipeline project is named the Pakistan Stream Gas Pipeline (PSGP).

      It was formerly known as the North-South gas pipeline, and sometimes as PakSteam.

      The project is designed to transport liquefied natural gas from Karachi to Kasur.

      90

      What is the primary reason behind the Iran-Pakistan gas pipeline project?

      Correct Answer: To export Iranian natural gas to meet Pakistan's energy needs
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The pipeline is intended to supply Iranian natural gas to Pakistan to overcome its severe electricity and gas shortages.

      It strengthens bilateral economic relations by providing Pakistan with a sustainable energy source and Iran with a stable export market.

      91

      Mir Chakar Khan Rind belonged to which ethnic group?

      Correct Answer: Baloch
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Name: Chakar Khan Rind (1468–1565)

      Native Name: میر چاکَر خان رِند (Balochi)

      Role: Baloch chieftain

      Achievement: Founded the Second Baloch Confederacy (1487–1512)

      Legacy:

      • Considered a folk hero of the Baloch people
      • Important figure in the Baloch epic Hani and Sheh Mureed

      Which countries established Regional Cooperation for Development (RCD) in the year 1964?

      Correct Answer: Iran, Pakistan and Turkey
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      RCD was established on 21 July 1964

      It was dissolved in 1979

      RCD stands for Regional Cooperation for Development

      They established by Iran, Pakistan,and Turkey

      Its headquarters were located in Iran

      What is the Height of Tirich Mir peak?

      Correct Answer: 7708 meters
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Tirich Mir is the highest mountain in the Hindu Kush range

      It’s the 33rd-highest peak in the world.

      It is the highest mountain in the world

      Tirich Mir is located in the Chitral District of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa

      Tirich Mir height: 7708 meters 

      Its first recorded climber on 21 July 1950 by a Norwegian


      Additional Information:

      Pakistan Highest Peaks List:

      Himalayas range: Nanga Parbat

      K-2: Krakarm range

      Kirthar: Zardak peak

      Koh e Suleman: Takhat e Sulaimani

      Salt range: Sakesar

      98

      What was the capital of Pakistan immediately after partition in 1947?

      Correct Answer: Karachi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      After the partition of British India in 1947, the capital of Pakistan was Karachi.

      In 1963, Islamabad was established as the new capital of Pakistan, replacing Karachi.

      Reasons for Shifting Capital: Islamabad's location in the northern part of the country provided better accessibility to all regions of Pakistan, unlike Karachi

      99

      Who won Pakistan's first medal in Islamic Solidarity Games 2025?

      Correct Answer: Fatima Zahra
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Boxer Fatima Zahra confirmed the first medal for Pakistan at the 6th Islamic Solidarity Games taking place in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia in November 2025.

      She qualified for the -60 kg semifinals on 6 Nov 2025 night as she defeated her Algerian opponent, Hamda Melissa, 5-0 in the quarterfinal bout.

      She confirmed a bronze medal.

      She belongs to Sargodha and has been boxing since 2018.

      She is a Pakistan Army athlete.

      ***

      100

      Name the Pakistani shooters who won silver and bronze medal in Asian Shooting Grand Prix 2025 held in Kuwait?

      Correct Answer: Usman Chand & Imam Haroon
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Usman Chand clinched the silver medal, while Imam Haroon secured bronze.

      ****

      101

      Name the constable of Lahore police who won Gold Medal in World Fitness Federation Championship 2025?

      Correct Answer: Asim Raza
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Lahore Police constable Asim Raza has brought pride to Pakistan by winning a gold medal and a Pro Card at the World Fitness Federation (WFF) Universe 2025 Championship held in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia.

      Asim Raza excelled in the Amateur Men’s Classic Bodybuilding Performance category, defeating contestants from South Africa, Russia and India to claim the gold medal. 


      27 countries participated in this competition. 

      102

      Which City of Pakistan was destroyed in the Earthquake of 1935?

      Correct Answer: Quetta
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • On 31 May 1936 Quetta was devasted by an earthquake;
      • ****
      • An earthquake occurred on 31 May 1935
      • An earthquake occurred between 2:33 am and 3:40 am at Quetta.
      • The causalities were up to 60,000
      • ***

      Related Question to Earthquake:

      • Attabad lake emerged in the Hunza valley due to an Earthquake
      • A powerful earthquake killed at least 1000 people and left scores injured in Afghanistan in the early hours on 22 June 2022.
      • Who came to Pakistan after the earthquake as a special representative of UNO's earthquake relief? George Bush Sr.
      • Alaska U.S. state has had the most earthquakes
      • Which two countries witnessed a massive earthquake on October 30, 2020? Turkey and Greece
      • Which province was mostly hit by an earthquake in Afghanistan in June 2022? Paktika
      •  ***
      • An earthquake is a Natural Disaster
      • An activity related to an earthquake is Seismic activity
      • An earthquake can be simplified as a Shaking of the earth
        The intensity of an earthquake is measured by: the Richter scale
      • The earthquake had a magnitude of 7.7 Mw
      • ****
      • Richter Scale was developed in 1935.
      • Seismology is the study of earthquakes.
      103

      Pakistan conducted nuclear tests on May 28, 1998 at?

      Correct Answer: Chaghi Hills
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan conducted a series of nuclear tests on 28 May 1998

      conducted at the Chagai test site, in Balochistan.

      5 Nuclear tests were conducted at 15:15 hrs PST on 28 May 1998.

      The tests were performed at Ras Koh Hills in the Chagai District 

      it was Pakistan's first public test of nuclear weapons.

      while 

      Chagai II

      Chagai-II is the codename assigned to the second atomic test conducted by Pakistan, carried out on 30 May 1998

      These tests followed the Chagai-I series

      The test site was Kharan Desert, Pakistan

      The number of tests is 1

      ND19-3-2023

      104

      The Sindh Provincial Museum is located in ____?

      Correct Answer: Hyderabad
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Sindh Museum was establish in 1971 to collect, preserve, study, and exhibit the records of the cultural history of Sindh and Indus Valley Civilization.
      105

      Which of the following organization has both India and Pakistan as its member? ASEAN, ECO, SAARC, None of these

      Correct Answer: SAARC
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      SAARC stands for South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation

      SAARC Secretariat was founded by Bangladeshi diplomat Abul Ahsan

      The SAARC movement was launched for Regional cooperation

      SAARC was Founded in Dhaka: on December 8, 1985

      Headquarters: Kathmandu
      *****

      SAARC cooperation has given observer status to 9 states:

      1. Australia,
      2. China,
      3. the European Union,
      4. Iran,
      5. Japan,
      6. Mauritius,
      7. Myanmar,
      8. South Korea
      9. and the United States

      *******

      There is a total of eight members of SAARC;

      Remember Hints: MBBS PAIN
      1. Afghanistan,
      2. Bangladesh,
      3. Bhutan,
      4. India,
      5. Nepal,
      6. the Maldives,
      7. Pakistan
      8. and Sri Lanka
      106

      Which of the following does not border with Sindh? Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Balochistan, Arabian Sea, Punjab

      Correct Answer: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Borders of Sindh:

      Sindh is bordered by:

      • Balochistan to the west and north-west
      • Punjab to the north
      • Arabian Sea to the south
      • Indian states (Rajasthan and Gujarat) to the east

      No direct border:

      Khyber Pakhtunkhwa does not share a direct border with Sindh; Punjab lies between them.

      Correct Answer: Indus

      It is located in Mianwali District, Punjab. 

      Chashma Barrage Construction began1967

      Chashma Barrage Opening date 1971

      Chashma Barrage Length is 1,084 m (3,556 ft)

      Correct Answer: 27.63 million tonnes

      This production level reflects challenges in the agriculture sector, affecting food security and export potential.

      What is the total area of Balochistan Province?

      Correct Answer: 347,190 Sq Km
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      "Baluchistan" important question and information:

      Baluchistan covers 42 percent of the area of Pakistan

      Baluchistan province is located in South West of Pakistan.

        Baluchistan became a province in 1970.

          Capital of Baluchistan is Quetta.

            Baluchistan is the biggest province of Pakistan by area.

            Balochistan is located on the Iranian plateau.

            Sajji dish belongs to Balochistan

            35 districts are there in Balochistan

            There are many rivers in Balochistan but 7 are the main

            347,190 sq Km is the total area of the Balochistan Province

            Hingol is the largest river in the Balochistan

            Baluchistan province has 7 divisions.

                          112

                          The 1973 Constitution of Pakistan came into force on?

                          Correct Answer: 14th August 1973
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                             
                          • The 1973 Constitution of Pakistan was Passed on April 10, 1973
                          • Approved on April 12
                          • Enforced on August 14, 1973
                          • Drafted by Zulfikar Ali Bhutto's government,
                          • This Constitution is called the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan 1973
                          Additional information
                          Feature of the 1973 Constitution
                          (Majlis-e-Shoora) consisting of the National Assembly and the Senate.
                          Declares Islam as the state religion
                          Requires all laws to conform to the Quran and Sunnah.

                          Last updated on 09-03-2026


                          113

                          Indus water treaty was signed between _____?

                          Correct Answer: India and Pakistan
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • The Indus Waters Treaty, signed on September 19, 1960, between India and Pakistan and brokered by the World Bank.
                            • It allocates the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan and the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India.
                            • It was signed in Karachi by Pakistan President Ayub Khan, Indian Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru, and Mr. W.A.B. Illif. 
                            • The Treaty became effective on April 1, 1960.
                            114

                            In 1916, where was the joint session of the All India Muslim League and the Indian National Congress held?

                            Correct Answer: Lucknow
                            Show explanation
                            Explanation
                            • The Lucknow Pact was an agreement between the Indian National Congress (INC) and the All-India Muslim League in December 1916.
                              • Led by Bal Gangadhar Tilak for the INC and Muhammad Ali Jinnah for the Muslim League.
                              • The Lucknow Pact allowed representation for religious minorities in provincial legislatures.
                              • It was adopted by the Congress on December 29 and by the League on December 31, 1916.
                              115

                              The government of Benazir Bhutto was dismissed for the second time on November 1996 by _____?

                              Correct Answer: President of Pakistan Farooq Leghari
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Farooq Ahmad Khan Leghari was the eighth president of Pakistan

                              eighth president of Pakistan from 14 November 1993 until resigning on 2 December 1997.

                              He was the first Baloch to be elected as president.

                              He started his own Millat party 

                              In 1996 he dismissed Benazir Government

                              Born: Sardar Farooq Ahmad Khan Leghari 29 May 1940

                              Died: 20 October 2010 (aged 70) Rawalpindi, Pakistan

                              Resting place: Choti Zareen, Dera Ghazi Khan, Pakistan

                              Political party: Pakistan Muslim League (Q) (2002–2010)

                              116

                              Which country uses GMT+5 as its standard time?

                              Correct Answer: Pakistan
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • Pakistan's standard time is 5 hours from Greenwich means Time
                              • ***
                              • Pakistan's standard time was suggested by Professor Muhammad Anwar in 1951
                              • **
                              • Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) is the standard time of which of UK
                              117

                              +92 is the country code of _____?

                              Correct Answer: Pakistan
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              The County codes were introduced in 1960 by International Telecon Union.
                              PK is the national code of Pakistan.
                              +92 is the dialing code of Pakistan. 
                              118

                              Which of the following languages has the most alphabets? English, Urdu, Sindhi, None of these

                              Correct Answer: Sindhi
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The Sindhi language, which is based on a modified Arabic script, has 52 alphabetic characters. 

                              The English alphabet has 26 letters.

                              The Urdu alphabet has 39 letters.

                              119

                              Who was the first democratically elected female prime minister of Pakistan?

                              Correct Answer: Benazir Bhutto
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Benazir Bhutto became the first female prime minister of Pakistan in 1988

                              As Prime Minister first time from 1988 to 1990 

                               As Prime Minister second time from 1993 to 1996.

                              She was the first woman to head a democratic government in a Muslim-majority nation.

                              Benazir Bhutto was a Pakistani politician who served as the 11th and 13th prime ministers of Pakistan 

                              Bhutto Pakistan's second nationally elected Prime Minister.

                              Benazir Bhutto was born on June 21, 1953

                              Benazir Bhutto died On 8 February 2008

                              Benazir Bhutto married Asif Ali Zardari on December 18, 1987 

                              Awards: United Nations Prize in the Field of Human Rights

                              ND28-4-2023



                              120

                              Who was the first person to use the term Two Nation Theory in 1867?

                              Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmed Khan
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Sir Syed is considered as the first person to theorize the idea of separate nationhood for Muslims in subcontinent.

                              In a speech at Meerut in 1866 he presented on overall scenario of post colonial phase in

                              which he described Muslims and Hindus as two nations

                              Syed Ahmad Khan who presented the theory of the two nations 

                              He presented this theory In a speech at Meerut in 1866 

                              The two-nation theory is the basis of the creation of Pakistan.


                              The theory advocated by the All India Muslim League is the founding principle of the Pakistan Movement.

                              The state of India officially rejected the two-nation theory


                              Aga Khan

                              He called on the British Raj to consider Muslims to be a separate nation from India,

                              the so-called 'Two Nation Theory"

                              Abdul

                              Ghaffar

                              khan

                              He founded the Khudai Khidmatgar commonly known as the RED SHIRTS,

                              during the 1920s

                              Awarded Bharat Ratna in 1987

                              Abbas 

                              Tyabji

                              He is also known as the Grand Old Man of Gujarat

                              Served as the Chief Justice of Baroda State

                              Tyabji launched the Dharasana Satyagraha. in 1930



                              agm19.3.23


                              121

                              The Lucknow Pact was adopted by the Congress in

                              Correct Answer: December 1916
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              On 1st October 1906, a Muslim political delegation led by Aga Khan

                              Met with the Viceroy of India to demand a separate electorate for Muslims.

                              This delegation played a crucial role in the establishment of the Muslim League in 1906

                              122

                              Quaid-e-Azam presented famous ________ in reply of Nehru report.

                              Correct Answer: 14 points
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The Nehru Report was published on August 28, 1928, at the Lucknow conference of all the parties.

                              the draft constitution was prepared which was called the “Nehru Committee Report“.

                              Jinnah Voted against this report.

                              And represented 14 points in the reply to Nehru Report

                              List of Jinnah's fourteen points (presents in 1929 in response of )

                              Title                               Description
                              Point 1:Federal System
                              Point 2:Provincial Autonomy
                              Point 3:Representation of Minorities
                              Point 4:Number of Muslim Representative
                              Point 5:Separate Electorates
                              Point 6:Muslim Majority Provinces
                              Point 7:Religious Liberty
                              Point 8:Three-Fourth Representation
                              Point 9:Separation of Sindh
                              Point 10:Introduction of Reforms in N.W.F.P and Baluchistan
                              Point 11:Government Services
                              Point 12:Protection of Muslim’s culture and Language
                              Point 13:One-Third Muslim Ministers
                              Point 14:

                              Constitution

                              123

                              Who opposed the separate electorate?

                              Correct Answer: Congress and other Hindu bodies
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              All important Question Related to " Separate Electorate" 

                              Indian Councils Act of 1909, also called Morley-Minto Reforms

                              Minto-Morley Reforms Muslim's demand for a separate electorate was incorporated

                              In 12 March 1909 the Morley-Minto Changes allowed partitioned electorates to Muslims.

                              This Act further extended democratic participation by increasing the size of the legislative councils, both Central and provincial.

                              27 out of add up to 60 members were to be chosen and 5 seats were saved for the Muslims.

                              Indian Council Act 1909 separate electorate for Muslims was accepted

                              on 28 August 1928 Nehru Report rejected the demand for a separate electorate

                              Congress and other Hindu bodies opposed the separate electorate

                              ND30-4-2023

                              124

                              In which year M.A.O College became University?

                              Correct Answer: 1920
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College was founded by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan in 1875, initially as a primary school, with the intention of turning it into a college-level institution.
                              • In 1877, the school was raised to college level.
                              • Lord Lytton, the Viceroy of India at the time, laid the foundation stone of the Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College (MAO College) on January 8, 1878. The college was later upgraded to Aligarh Muslim University (AMU) in 1920.

                              He served as India’s first Education Minister post-1947.

                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              132

                              The Article 256 is?

                              Correct Answer: Private armies
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Article 256 of the Constitution of Pakistan forbids the formation of any private organization

                              That can function as a military organization.

                              This means such organizations are illegal and any that are formed will be considered unlawful.

                              This article is a part of the country's constitution, which was adopted on April 10, 1973.

                              Write the following word stands for:NUTECH

                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              It as an affiliate university of the Frontier Works Organization (FWO), a department of Pakistan Army.

                              Motto

                              رہبرِ ترقی و کمال

                              OGRA stands for _____?

                              Correct Answer: Oil and Gas Regulatory Authority
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              OGRA stands for Oil and Gas Regulatory Authority.

                              It is the regulatory body responsible for overseeing the oil and gas sector in Pakistan.

                              It was established in 2002.

                              It to ensure a fair and transparent regulatory framework for the exploration, production, transportation, and distribution of oil and gas resources.

                              137

                              PPI stands for ______?

                              Correct Answer: Pakistan Press International
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                            • Pakistan Press International (PPI) was established in June 1956.
                            • It was initially founded as the Pakistan Press Association (PPA).
                              • 138

                              IBCC stands for _____?

                              Correct Answer: Inter Board Coordination Commission
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                            • IBCC (Inter Board Coordination Commission) is a federal education body in Pakistan established in 1972.
                            • Key responsibilities include standardizing exams, recognizing local and foreign qualifications, and promoting curricular and extracurricular activities.
                            • Advises and facilitates teacher and student exchanges and represents the Higher Education Commission (HEC).
                              • 139

                              "K" in Pakistan stands for ______?

                              Correct Answer: Kashmir
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The word was first introduced now or never in 1933 by Choudhry Rahmat Ali.

                              The "Pakistan Declaration" titled Now or Never means Are We to Live or Perish Forever?

                              In this pamphlet, the word Pakistan was used for the first time 

                              This pamphlet was derived from the two-nation theory of Muslim reformer Syed Ahmed Khan

                              it was a pamphlet, referring to the names of the five northern regions of British India: 

                              1. Punjab
                              2. Afghanistan
                              3. Kashmir
                              4. Sindh
                              5. Baluchistan. (Pakistan)

                              NAB stands for ______?

                              Correct Answer: National Accountability Bureau
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The National Accountability Bureau (NAB) is Pakistan's lead anti-corruption agency.

                              It established by Pervez Musharraf on November 16, 1999.

                              Its headquarters are in Islamabad.

                              It focuses on combating political corruption and ensuring accountability.
